summary:
Thin bank-research window: just 5 of 60 tracked tweets carried a bank/strategist tag; the remaining 55 were untagged general-market chart posts (mostly Mike Zaccardi, plus HFI Research, LanceRoberts, TheChartReport, and dailychartbook) dropped per filter. No @neilksethi / @neilksethinew posts appeared in this collection cycle.

Goldman Sachs leads with 2 items: hyperscaler AI capex return economics, and a record-low S&P put/call skew flagging complacent crash-protection demand.

Apollo (Torsten Slok) contributes 1 item on oil and yields decoupling, signaling the Fed's inflation problem has shifted from headline to core.

BofA contributes 1 item (via @eliant_capital) on crowded consensus positioning.

JPMorgan contributes 1 item (via @dailychartbook) on record operating-margin expansion, seen at 54% and forecast toward 70% by year-end.

**By bank:** Goldman 2, Apollo/Slok 1, BofA 1, JPM 1.

banks.theme: Goldman Sachs -- hyperscaler AI capex payback economics and record-low equity crash-protection demand

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: GS: Hyperscaler AI investment is unprecedented, requiring downstream profit pool capture to justify returns https://t.co/YywmOWgMtO

items.author: dailychartbook
items.text:
"The S&P put/call skew just collapsed to 0.71. Not a low. The lowest reading on record ... crash protection is essentially free. Nobody wants it."

Goldman Sachs via @ThierryBorgeat https://t.co/d2zwczE4fQ

banks.theme: Apollo (Torsten Slok) -- oil-yield decoupling flags the Fed's inflation problem shifting from headline to core

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text:
When oil and yields stop moving together, it signals the Fed's problem has shifted from headline to core... cheaper oil alone won't open the door to cuts

-Torsten at Apollo https://t.co/FlNpNS4Ygh

banks.theme: BofA -- crowded consensus positioning ('one side of the boat')

items.author: eliant_capital
items.text: Everyone’s back on one side of the boat… https://t.co/PQVCo1FUl5

banks.theme: JPMorgan -- record operating margin expansion, 54% now vs. 70% forecast by year-end

items.author: dailychartbook
items.text:
Memory: "Last quarter, operating margins hit 54% vs. a previous peak of 34%, and they’re expected to increase to 70% by the end of the year."

-JPMorgan https://t.co/vhiay8ZA70
